rationale: Neither candidate hits the two actual GT concerns precisely (docstring staleness and legacy-path ordering appear already resolved in the final diff, making them hard to rediscover); X's 'unconditional containment fallback' comment and Y's 'affects all speech models, not just Voxtral' finding both graze the same underlying theme as GT's comment 1. Y goes deeper with well-verified, grounded findings (notably a genuine word-boundary substring-containment bug proven with a concrete counterexample, plus a dead-code guard and a missing precedence test) and gives more concrete actionable fixes/tests to add, while X's two findings are solid but thinner. Y's 'major' severity tag on the cross-model test-coverage ask is slightly inflated but not fabricated.
